decks.notes: The deck focuses on playing aggressive creatures early in the game to put pressure on the opponent, while also disrupting their strategy with cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. Collected Company allows for explosive creature plays, while Skyclave Apparition provides removal for problematic threats. The mana base is optimized for fast and consistent mana production, with Boseiju, Who Endures providing protection for key spells. Elspeth, Storm Slayer and The Wandering Emperor offer late-game power and resilience. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a fast and efficient creature-based strategy.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly overwhelm your opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells, utilizing the red and blue color combination to deal damage efficiently. By playing low-cost creatures and instant-speed spells, you can apply pressure early in the game while also disrupting your opponent's game plan with counterspells and bounce effects. The deck aims to maintain a steady stream of threats while controlling the board and tempo of the game, ultimately leading to a swift victory through relentless aggression and calculated decision-making.

The deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate large amounts of mana quickly, enabling powerful combo plays. It includes cards that can untap or produce additional mana from the Lotus Field, as well as key combo pieces like Ultimatum','','');">Emergent Ultimatum and Omniscience to overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells. Arboreal Grazer and Sylvan Scrying help to find and ramp into Lotus Field, while Hidden Strings and Pore Over the Pages provide card advantage and filtering. The deck aims to assemble its combo pieces efficiently and win through overwhelming spells and mana advantage.

This deck focuses on disrupting opponents with hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress while controlling the board with removal spells like Abrupt Decay and Fatal Push. The deck also utilizes powerful creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to apply pressure and close out the game. The mana base is designed to support an efficient curve and consistent access to the deck's key spells. Ultimately, this deck aims to out-value and out-last opponents through a combination of disruption, removal, and powerful threats.

The focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana using cards like Arboreal Grazer and Crumbling Vestige to play Lotus Field, which allows for explosive plays with cards like Scapeshift and Formidable Speaker. Aftermath Analyst and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods provide card advantage and utility, while support cards like Port of Karfell and Glasspool Mimic help to control the battlefield. The deck aims to establish a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent with powerful combo plays, utilizing the unique synergies of the Lotus Field archetype.

The primary focus of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to establish a fast and aggressive board presence through efficient creatures like Archon of Emeria, Enduring Innocence, and Ouroboroid. With mana acceleration from Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents early on while disrupting their game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben. The inclusion of powerful finishers like Skyclave Apparition and Elspeth, Storm Slayer ensures that the deck can maintain pressure and close out games quickly. The deck's mana base is carefully constructed to support its aggressive strategy while also providing flexibility for casting spells with multiple colored requirements.

This Selesnya Aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and disruptive spells. By playing early mana dorks like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic, the deck can quickly build up its board presence and cast powerful threats like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Enduring Innocence slow down the opponent's game plan, while Collected Company provides card advantage and helps to refill the board. Elspeth, Storm Slayer serves as a finisher, while cards like Ouroboroid and Brushland provide additional utility and flexibility.

The deck focuses on maximizing the efficiency of low-cost creatures and spells to overwhelm the opponent quickly. By utilizing mana dorks like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves, the deck aims to ramp into powerful threats such as Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition. Cards like Collected Company help to dig for more creatures while providing additional board presence. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Enduring Innocence disrupt the opponent's game plan, while Elspeth, Storm Slayer and The Wandering Emperor provide additional aggression and finishers. The deck's mana base is optimized for consistency and speed.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate powerful creatures and overwhelm the opponent. It includes various protection spells and removal options to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The inclusion of Thoughtseize allows for hand disruption, while the Monument to Endurance helps to stabilize the board. The deck also utilizes mana fixing lands and additional support cards like Fleeting Spirit and Guardian of New Benalia to enhance its overall performance.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on overwhelming the opponent with quick creature drops and disruption spells. By utilizing low-cost creatures like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves, the deck aims to ramp into powerful threats like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben helps to slow down the opponent's plays, while Collected Company provides additional creature synergy. The deck also includes removal spells like Enduring Innocence and access to powerful planeswalkers like Elspeth, Storm Slayer. With a mix of aggro pressure and disruption, this deck aims to close out games quickly.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to slow down the opponent's game plan by utilizing a variety of disruptive spells and abilities. This deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Go for the Throat, while also using cards like Narset, Parter of Veils and Cryogen Relic to limit the opponent's resources. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for extra value by blinking permanents like Stock Up and Hopeless Nightmare. Overall, this deck excels at outlasting opponents and grinding out wins through careful resource management and control.

The primary focus of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures like Archon of Emeria, Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, and Skyclave Apparition. The deck also utilizes mana acceleration from cards like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic to quickly deploy threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Collected Company allows for additional creature threats to be put into play at instant speed. The deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent while also having some resilience and disruptive elements with cards like Enduring Innocence and The Wandering Emperor.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on playing efficient creatures and disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria. It aims to pressure the opponent early with creatures like Llanowar Elves and Ouroboroid, and can generate card advantage with Collected Company. The deck also includes powerful cards like Skyclave Apparition and Elspeth, Storm Slayer to deal with threats. The mana base is configured to support the aggro strategy, with a mix of untapped dual lands and utility lands like Eiganjo, Seat of the Empire and Boseiju, Who Endures.

The focus of this deck is to quickly play spells and creatures that benefit from casting multiple spells in a turn, specifically Arclight Phoenix. By including low-cost spells like Consider, Opt, and Sleight of Hand, as well as powerful cards like Treasure Cruise and Lightning Axe, the deck aims to fill up the graveyard with spells and bring back Arclight Phoenix for repeated hits. The mana base is optimized for a balanced mix of blue and red sources to ensure consistent casting of spells and creatures.

The main idea behind this deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan while gradually building up your own board presence. By using a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and secure the win. The deck has a strong mana base to support its powerful spells and creatures, allowing for flexibility in casting the necessary spells at the right time. The key strategy is to grind out the opponent through attrition and resource management.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive strategy that utilizes low-cost creatures and powerful support cards. By quickly building a board presence with cards like Llanowar Elves and Elvish Mystic, the deck aims to apply early pressure and disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Skyclave Apparition. The inclusion of Collected Company allows for additional creature summoning and boosts the deck's overall consistency. Boseiju, Who Endures and Elspeth, Storm Slayer provide additional versatility and control options to help maintain dominance on the battlefield.
